From there, a "push up" will place the box on the target. Avoid oscillating by prioritizing this path over previous rightward movements.

experiences.intentions: The player is currently at (1,2) and has been attempting to push the box at (2,1) upward without success. This indicates a positioning error. To push the box at (2,1) onto the target at (1,1), the player must first move left to (1,1) (a target location), positioning themselves directly above the box. From there, a "push up" action would move the box to (1,1), solving one target. The recent repeated "push up" attempts failed because the player was not adjacent to the box in the correct direction.

experiences.intentions: The box at (4,1) can be pushed into target (4,2) by first moving left to position adjacent to it. The player is currently at (4,4), so moving left twice will reach (4,2), allowing a right push to place the box on the target. This avoids deadlocks and directly addresses one of the two targets.
